conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex who is 7, Blake who is 12, and Charlie who is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine eligibility for Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10).

Let me start by asking: **Are you responsible for each of these children? By this I mean, do they live with you and are you the person who is primarily looking after them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, they do all live with me and I look after them, but I should mention that I don't actually live in the UK - I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's an important detail as Child Benefit generally requires a connection to the UK.

**Can you tell me which country you're currently living in, and are you or have you been working in that country?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in France at the moment. Yes, I am working here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since you're living and working in France, this does affect Child Benefit eligibility as it's generally only available to people living in the UK or in specific circumstances involving work abroad.

Let me ask a few more questions to get the full picture:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) currently paying UK National Insurance contributions, or are you employed by a UK-based employer while working in France?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not paying UK National Insurance contributions, and I'm not employed by a UK-based employer. I work for a French company here in France.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Let me ask one more important question: **Are any of the children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie - currently living in the UK with someone else, or are all three of them living with you in France?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: All three of them are living with me here in France.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Based on what you've told me, I need to inform you of the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children** - Alex, Blake, or Charlie.

Here's why:

Child Benefit is generally only available to people who live in the UK. Since you are living and working in France with all three children, and you're employed by a French company (not a UK employer) and not paying UK National Insurance contributions, you don't meet the residence requirements for claiming Child Benefit.

While you may be eligible for similar family benefits through the French social security system, UK Child Benefit is not available in your current circumstances. If your situation changes in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK with your children - you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about this decision?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
